meaning: n.搬弄是非者,告密的人,证据adj.搬弄是非的,泄密的,警告的
examples: The telltale smell of cigarettes told her that he had been in the room.
Only occasionally did the telltale redness around his eyes betray the fatigue he was suffering.
The handle of the telltale tea-spoon was visible under the bed-valance.
Check-signing authority is a telltale sign of responsible personhood, unless it is not exercised.
One telltale sign, Bathgate says, is the difference in the average contributions for the two Democrats.
